_comment: GLaDOS v3.1 — CWE cascade weightings for dynamic replanning. When a validator writes a finding with confidence >= replan_threshold AND its CWE appears here, GLaDOS halts and proposes a replan biased toward enables_vectors.

CWE-94.name: Code Injection / RCE
CWE-94.rationale: RCE obsoletes further surface enumeration; pivot to post-exploitation.

CWE-78.name: OS Command Injection
CWE-78.rationale: Same logic as RCE; command exec is terminal surface-finding.

CWE-287.name: Authentication Bypass
CWE-287.rationale: Auth bypass unlocks previously-gated routes; re-run recon inside the authenticated perimeter before picking next vector.

CWE-918.name: SSRF
CWE-918.rationale: SSRF pivots to internal network surface — add cloud-metadata + internal recon, keep webapp chain.

CWE-89.name: SQL Injection
CWE-89.rationale: Stay in webapp chain but elevate data-exfil priority.

CWE-798.name: Hardcoded Credentials
CWE-798.rationale: Credentials pivot outward; trigger AD/postex paths.

CWE-22.name: Path Traversal
CWE-22.rationale: LFI often yields source code — schedule source-code agent in parallel.

CWE-284.name: Improper Access Control (IDOR)
CWE-284.rationale: IDOR without auth-bypass stays in current chain; escalate authz-probe depth.

CWE-502.name: Deserialization
CWE-502.rationale: Deserialization = likely RCE; dispatch poc-coder + postex.

CWE-611.name: XXE
CWE-611.rationale: XXE often leaks files + SSRF-adjacent; keep chain, add internal-recon.
